message:
fix(engine): harden hash stability and security across core modules

Prevent hash collision vulnerabilities by length-prefixing all variable-length
fields before hashing, and fix several security/correctness issues:

- cache_oblivious_metadata_substrate: use stable byte discriminants instead of
  Debug format for hash inputs, preventing hash instability across Rust versions
- expected_loss_selector: length-prefix action/state strings and prepend entry
  count to prevent concatenation collisions in loss matrix hashing
- proof_ingestion: replace naive H(key||msg) signature construction with proper
  AuthenticityHash keyed construction to prevent length-extension attacks; add
  #[serde(skip)] to signing_key field to prevent key leakage in serialization
- recovery_artifact: length-prefix proof and operator action fields in both
  ArtifactBuilder and RecoveryArtifactStore ID computation
- regret_bounded_router: widen Exp3 importance-weighted reward and log-weight
  delta computations to i128 to prevent integer overflow on extreme values
- rough_path_regime_geometry: length-prefix trace_id, signature_hash, and
  corridor from/to strings in topology rehashing
- semantic_flattening_inventory: length-prefix all variable-length fields and
  add explicit Option markers for line_hint presence/absence
- signature_drift_gate: length-prefix signature_id and feature map keys
- succinct_witness_compiler: introduce length-prefix helper closure for
  WitnessSchema ID and ProvenanceAttachment content hash computation; add
  epoch field and explicit Option marker for legal_summary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
